reasoning: The bottom row’s Assyrian compound sign matches the one referenced later in I3 as the way Nineveh is written; the English column for that row therefore takes the English name.
The compound sign for Nineveh is the juxtaposition of the signs for fish and house; the right-hand element is the house sign, so (a) is ‘house’.
The left-hand element of the Nineveh compound is the fish sign; thus the phrase is ‘house of fish’.
